designs.name: t1_a1_demand_shift
designs.mechanism: Hard demand handoff 7b->8b; peak occupancy 0.90. Hot-switch should follow the step within the fast loop while cold-start scaling lags; neither model drops its serving floor.

designs.name: t2_a2_anticorrelated
designs.mechanism: Anti-correlated 7b/8b, Sum(rho)~5.2 constant across 233s phases; donor/receiver rebalance (slow loop) should migrate capacity each phase without both models staying high; 14b never participates.

designs.name: t3_a3_io_drift
designs.mechanism: RPS held constant while dsqwen-7b output grows 128->512 (decode gets heavier). Queue length / KVCache signals lag, so APA reacts late; TSS weighted throughput reflects the rising load immediately, so TRE should scale earlier.

designs.name: t4_a4_spike_vs_burst
designs.mechanism: Narrow 17s spikes (shorter than the EMA time constant) must NOT trigger a full scale-up or churn; the 127s wide burst (> 3 slow loops) must. Also exercises SafeScale rollback.

designs.name: t5_a5_tp_pressure
designs.mechanism: dsqwen-14b (tp_size=2) ramps to peak occupancy 0.90 while 7b/8b hold single-GPU slots awake, forcing the allocator to defragment two-GPU placement without breaking the 1-slot models' serving floor.

designs.name: t6_a6_control
designs.mechanism: Gentle in-phase ramp across all models to peak occupancy ~0.575 (loose); no model sustains rho > 1.2 so it stays below the C2 non-triviality threshold. Fairness control: TRE and APA should tie here, proving the other traces' gaps are real.

designs.name: t7_a2b_anticorrelated_hot
designs.mechanism: Anti-correlated 7b/8b, Sum(rho)~6.4 constant across 233s phases; donor/receiver rebalance (slow loop) should migrate capacity each phase without both models staying high; 14b never participates.
